narrationText: The original code crashed at runtime because it blindly trusted the API by using a type assertion, resulting in a TypeError when trying to read properties from a null value. This was fixed by switching the return type to unknown to acknowledge the unpredictable nature of external data, followed by a type guard that verifies the object structure before accessing its properties. If the data validation fails, the code now safely switches to a fallback guest message instead of throwing an error.
